text: Bought an umbrella that broke on day one — turns out it was waterproof to my disappointment, not the rain.
Ordered a 'silent' laptop fan. My neighbors now know all my browsing habits.
The 'fast charger' got my phone from 20% to 22% in an hour. Truly revolutionary technology.
Booked a 'quiet boutique hotel' next to a nightclub. Slept like a baby — one that cries all night.
The self-cleaning oven cleaned itself so thoroughly it cleaned right off the warranty.
Ordered the 'space-saving' folding bike — the only space it saves is in my hallway, where it now permanently lives because I can't actually fold it.
Got the 'unbreakable' phone case. Phone is fine. The case has a crack right down the middle.
Bought 'premium noise-cancelling' earbuds. The only noise they don't cancel is their own static.
The smart fridge was so smart it ordered me 47 cartons of oat milk while I was on vacation.
Subscribed to the 'lose weight without exercise' app. Lost €30 a month and gained nothing else.
Oh fantastic, another streaming service that needs three remotes and a PhD to navigate. Just what I needed.
Wow, $18 for a soggy salad with two croutons. Steal of the century, really.
Customer service was SO helpful — only took 47 minutes on hold to be told they couldn't help me.
Sure, charge me extra for 'premium' seats with two inches more legroom. Best $80 I've ever spent.
Love how the app updates every single day with new bugs. Keeps life exciting.
Brilliant move, putting the power button on the bottom of the laptop. Truly user-centric design.
Oh excellent, the WiFi router needs to be unplugged twice a day. Adds such a fun ritual to my morning.
Just lovely — dish arrived cold, late, and missing the main ingredient. Five stars all around.
Of course the printer ran out of colour ink to print a black-and-white document. Modern engineering at its finest.
Amazing how my €200 'waterproof' watch lasted exactly one shower. Worth every cent.
This vacuum doesn't suck — and I mean it doesn't pick up anything off the carpet.
Not the worst pizza I've ever had, but I wouldn't order it again.
The hotel wasn't dirty, wasn't loud, wasn't uncomfortable. Just nothing memorable either.
I can't say I disliked the movie, but I won't be recommending it to friends.
No issues with shipping, no problems with packaging, no complaints really — except the product itself didn't work.
The chair isn't unstable and the cushion isn't uncomfortable — but I wouldn't say I love it either.
Doesn't deliver on speed, doesn't deliver on accuracy, doesn't deliver on price. Skip it.
Not bad, not great. Just exists.
Instructions weren't clear, parts weren't labelled, and the end result wasn't what I expected.
I don't hate the colour, and the size isn't wrong — but the fabric? No way I'm keeping this.
Great battery life and a beautiful screen, but the keyboard is mushy and it overheats after 20 minutes.
Food was outstanding, service was painfully slow, and the place smelled like bleach the whole time.
The actors were incredible, the cinematography stunning, but the script felt like it was written in a weekend.
Comfortable bed and friendly staff, although the bathroom was tiny and the walls paper-thin.
Love the design and the price, hate the durability — broke within a month of light use.
Excellent picture quality and a fair price — but the remote feels like a children's toy and the menus are unintuitive.
Loved the room and the breakfast was incredible. Check-in took an hour and the parking cost more than the room itself.
Quick shipping, perfect packaging, exactly what I ordered — pity the product itself stopped working after a week.
Stylish, lightweight, and fits perfectly. Zipper broke after three uses.
Coffee was great, atmosphere was lovely, and prices were fair — but the staff was openly rude to a customer beside me.
Setup was a nightmare and the manual is in three languages I don't speak — but once it's running, this little espresso machine is fantastic.
The driver was 40 minutes late and clearly grumpy. The food, though, was probably the best Thai I've had this year.
Boring lobby, lukewarm welcome, weirdly creaky elevator — but the room itself was huge, spotless, and the view was worth the trip.
First week I was ready to send it back: confusing UI, sluggish performance. Now I use it every day and can't imagine going without it.
Expensive, slightly noisy, and the design isn't winning any awards — but it just works, every single time.
